Path analysis: NCP → Biogeochemistry → Coral physiology

  • Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • View Source

Material Legacies / Indirect Effects — Limnology and Oceanography Revision

Published

April 18, 2026

Overview

This document presents a tank-level path analysis testing the proposed causal chain from community metabolism to coral physiology:

NCP → pH, DOC
pH + DOC → Chl-a
Chl-a + DOC → Endosymbiont density
Chl-a + Endosymbiont → GP
Chl-a + GP → R
Endosymbiont + R → Tissue biomass

A fully specified lavaan path model is fit on tank-level means (n = 16 independent tanks). Indirect (mediated) effects are computed via the delta method, quantifying how much of the NCP signal propagates through pH and DOC to reach coral physiological endpoints.

Setup

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(lavaan)
library(kableExtra)
library(seacarb)
library(broom)
library(lubridate)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(patchwork)

Data loading

Code 
metadata <- read_csv(here("Data", "MO24BEAST_Metadata_FULL.csv")) |>
  mutate(
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT,
                       levels = c("Control", "Algae_Dom", "Coral_Dom", "Rubble_Dom")),
    # Values above 30 mg cm⁻² are biologically implausible and likely reflect
    # retained skeleton or mucus during tissue homogenisation
    mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass)
  )
Code 
pHcalib  <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "TrisCalSummer2024.csv"))
pHData   <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "CarbonateChemistry.csv"))
TableID  <- read_csv(here("Data", "TableID.csv"))
DOC_data <- read_csv(here("Data", "DOC", "DOC_full_data.csv"))

# Per-calibration-date linear model of mV vs. temperature interpolates the
# Tris buffer voltage at each sample's in-lab temperature, then seacarb::pH()
# converts electrode mV to pH on the total scale
pHSlope <- pHcalib |>
  nest_by(TrisCalDate) |>
  mutate(fitpH = list(lm(mVTris ~ TTris, data = pHcalib))) |>
  reframe(broom::tidy(fitpH)) |>
  dplyr::select(TrisCalDate, term, estimate) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = term, values_from = estimate) |>
  right_join(pHData, by = "TrisCalDate") |>
  mutate(mVTris = TEMPINLAB * TTris + `(Intercept)`) |>
  drop_na(TEMPINSITU, mV) |>
  mutate(pH = pH(Ex = mV, Etris = mVTris, S = SALINITY, T = TEMPINLAB)) |>
  drop_na(TEMPINSITU, TEMPINLAB, SALINITY, pH) |>
  # Correct from lab-temperature pH to in-situ pH; ALK = 2200 µmol/kg is a
  # fixed estimate sufficient for this temperature correction (not used for
  # carbonate chemistry calculations below)
  mutate(pH_insitu = pHinsi(
    pH = pH, ALK = 2200, Tinsi = TEMPINSITU, Tlab = TEMPINLAB,
    S = SALINITY, Pt = 0.1, k1k2 = "m10", kf = "dg"
  )) |>
  dplyr::select(-pH) |> rename(pH = pH_insitu) |>
  ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select(-c(mV, TrisCalDate, TTris, `(Intercept)`, mVTris))

DIC_calc <- pHSlope |>
  drop_na(pH, TA) |>
  mutate(TA_mol_kg = TA / 1e6)

# flag = 8: pH + TA as inputs — fully constrains the carbonate system.
# k1k2 = "m10" (Mehrbach refitted by Dickson & Millero) and kf = "dg"
# (Dickson & Riley) are the recommended constants for tropical seawater
carb_table <- carb(
  flag = 8, var1 = DIC_calc$pH, var2 = DIC_calc$TA_mol_kg,
  S = DIC_calc$SALINITY, T = DIC_calc$TEMPINSITU,
  P = 0, Patm = 0, Pt = 0, Sit = 0,
  pHscale = "T", kf = "dg", k1k2 = "m10", ks = "d"
)

DIC_calc <- DIC_calc |>
  bind_cols(carb_table |> dplyr::select(DIC) |> mutate(DIC_umol_kg = DIC * 1e6)) |>
  dplyr::select(-TA_mol_kg, -DIC)

pHSlope2 <- pHSlope |>
  left_join(DIC_calc |> dplyr::select(any_of(names(pHSlope)), DIC_umol_kg),
            by = intersect(names(pHSlope), names(DIC_calc))) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = ifelse(is.na(TREATMENT), "Inflow", TREATMENT)) |>
  full_join(DOC_data |> dplyr::select(-DATETIME),
            by = intersect(names(pHSlope), names(DOC_data))) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(NPOC_mg_L, TN_mg_L))

InflowData <- pHSlope2 |>
  filter(TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(FLOW_LEFT, FLOW_RIGHT, Notes, DO_MG_L, SALINITY, TEMPINSITU)) |>
  rename(pH_inflow = pH, TA_inflow = TA,
         DIC_inflow = DIC_umol_kg, DOC_inflow = NPOC_uM) |>
  mutate(INFLOW_TABLE = ifelse(TANK_NUM == "Inflow1", 1, 2)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select(DATE, TIME, INFLOW_TABLE, pH_inflow, TA_inflow, DIC_inflow, DOC_inflow)

# Benthic surface area of each mesocosm in cm² (22.5 × 22.5 cm tile footprint)
SurfaceArea <- 22.5 * 22.5

Data <- pHSlope2 |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(!TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)) |>
  left_join(TableID |> dplyr::select(TANK_NUM, INFLOW_TABLE), by = "TANK_NUM") |>
  left_join(InflowData, by = c("DATE", "TIME", "INFLOW_TABLE")) |>
  mutate(
    DATETIME       = ymd_hms(paste(DATE, TIME)),
    deltapH        = pH - pH_inflow,
    deltaDOC       = NPOC_uM - DOC_inflow,
    totalflow      = FLOW_RIGHT + FLOW_LEFT,
    # Residence time in hours: tank volume (10 L = 10,000 mL) ÷ flow (mL/min) ÷ 60
    residence_time = (1 / totalflow) * (10000 / 60),
    flowrate       = totalflow / 60,
    # Inflow minus outflow: positive deltaTA/deltaDIC = community uptake
    deltaTA        = TA_inflow - TA,
    deltaDIC       = DIC_inflow - DIC_umol_kg,
    # NEC via alkalinity anomaly: ÷2 for 2:1 TA:CaCO₃ stoichiometry;
    # ×1.025 for seawater density (kg/L); ×10 converts L to dm³ for flux scaling
    NEC = (deltaTA / 2) * 1.025 * 10 * (1 / residence_time) * (1 / SurfaceArea),
    # NEP via DIC anomaly, minus calcification signal (CaCO₃ precipitation
    # also consumes DIC independently of photosynthesis)
    NEP = (deltaDIC * 1.025 * 10 * (1 / residence_time) * (1 / SurfaceArea)) - NEC
  )

treatment_levels <- c("Control", "Algae_Dom", "Coral_Dom", "Rubble_Dom")

# Statistical outlier removal: Tukey outer fences (IQR × 3).
# Applied per variable, per time-point block (day and night separately).
# No hardcoded thresholds or treatment-specific rules.
remove_outliers <- function(x, multiplier = 3) {
  q   <- quantile(x, c(0.25, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
  iqr <- q[2] - q[1]
  ifelse(x < (q[1] - multiplier * iqr) |
         x > (q[2] + multiplier * iqr), NA, x)
}

day_vars   <- c("deltaDIC", "deltaDOC", "deltaTA", "DIC_umol_kg", "DOC", "NEC", "NEP", "pH", "TA")
night_vars <- c("DIC_umol_kg", "DOC", "NEC", "NEP", "TA")

chem_reframe_DAY_clean <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("12:00:00")) |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, DATE, TANK_NUM) |>
  dplyr::select(DATE, TIME, TANK_NUM, TREATMENT,
         TA, pH, DIC_umol_kg, NPOC_uM, deltapH, deltaTA, deltaDOC, deltaDIC, NEC, NEP) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(across(all_of(day_vars), remove_outliers))

chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("21:00:00")) |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, DATE, TANK_NUM) |>
  dplyr::select(DATE, TIME, TANK_NUM, TREATMENT,
         TA, pH, DIC_umol_kg, NPOC_uM, deltaTA, deltaDIC, NEC, NEP) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(across(all_of(night_vars), remove_outliers))
Code 
compute_fence_summary <- function(df, vars, time_label) {
  map_dfr(vars, function(v) {
    x   <- df[[v]]
    q   <- quantile(x, c(0.25, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
    iqr <- q[2] - q[1]
    lo  <- q[1] - 3 * iqr
    hi  <- q[2] + 3 * iqr
    n_removed <- sum((x < lo | x > hi), na.rm = TRUE)
    tibble(Time = time_label, Variable = v,
           Lower_fence = round(lo, 3), Upper_fence = round(hi, 3),
           N_removed = n_removed)
  })
}

day_raw <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("12:00:00")) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM)

night_raw <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("21:00:00")) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM)

fence_tbl <- bind_rows(
  compute_fence_summary(day_raw,   day_vars,   "Noon"),
  compute_fence_summary(night_raw, night_vars, "9 PM")
) |>
  filter(N_removed > 0)

fence_tbl |>
  kbl(col.names = c("Time", "Variable", "Lower fence", "Upper fence", "N removed"),
      caption = "Outlier fences (IQR × 3) and count of removed observations per variable per time point.") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
Statistical outlier removal summary (IQR × 3 outer fences). Each row shows the computed lower and upper fences for a variable at each time point, and how many observations were set to NA. Variables with zero removals are omitted.
Time Variable Lower fence Upper fence N removed
Noon deltaDIC -333.484 445.232 1
deltaDOC -78.534 84.799 15
deltaTA -164.029 208.992 1
DOC -75.528 329.301 2
NEC -1.184 1.480 7
NEP -3.051 4.263 4
pH 7.723 8.437 3
TA 2155.693 2509.678 3
9 PM DIC_umol_kg 1826.139 2214.077 1
DOC -47.220 258.161 2
NEC -0.633 0.670 11
NEP -2.126 1.972 1
TA 2223.767 2480.782 1
Note

Outlier removal method. Values are excluded using Tukey outer fences: observations falling below Q1 − 3 × IQR or above Q3 + 3 × IQR for each variable are set to NA. The 3× multiplier (rather than the standard 1.5×) corresponds to “far outliers” in Tukey’s original framework and is conservative enough to preserve genuine biological and biogeochemical variability while removing likely instrument errors or data entry anomalies. Fences are computed per variable per time point (noon and 9 PM separately) across all tanks and dates. No treatment-specific rules are applied.

Moorea backreef benthic community

Code 
pc_data <- read_csv(here("Data", "PercentCoverData", "backreef_benthic_comp_mean_perc.csv"),
                    show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(
    benthic_group = factor(benthic_group,
      levels = c("coral", "ctb", "CCA", "macroalgae", "sand"),
      labels = c("Coral", "Rubble", "Crustose Coralline Algae", "Macroalgae", "Sand")),
    year = factor(year)
  )

Figure

Code 
benthic_colors <- c(
  "Coral"                    = "#E07B54",
  "Crustose Coralline Algae" = "#A07850",
  "Macroalgae"               = "#2E8B57",
  "Rubble"                   = "#C8B89A",
  "Sand"                     = "#D6C8A8"
)

fig_moorea_cover <- ggplot(pc_data, aes(x = year, y = mean_percent_cover, fill = benthic_group)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill", stat = "identity", color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(mean_percent_cover >= 3,
                               paste0(round(mean_percent_cover, 0), "%"), "")),
            position = position_fill(vjust = 0.5),
            color = "white", size = 3.2, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = benthic_colors,
                    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Percent cover", fill = "Benthic category") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "right",
    legend.title       = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title         = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text          = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig0_moorea_percent_cover.png"),
       fig_moorea_cover, width = 5, height = 5, dpi = 300)

fig_moorea_cover

Biogeochemical differences by treatment

Before testing the mediation pathway, we first ask whether the four community treatments produced measurable differences in NCP, NCC, pH, and DOC. Measurements are examined separately at noon (12:00) and 9 PM (21:00) to distinguish daytime production and calcification signals from nighttime respiration dynamics. Mixed-effects models include tank identity as a random intercept to account for repeated daily measurements on the same tank (response ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM)).

Figure

Code 
library(multcomp)
library(multcompView)

trt_colors <- c("Control" = "#4682B4", "Algae_Dom" = "#2E8B57",
                "Coral_Dom" = "#E07B54", "Rubble_Dom" = "#A07850")
trt_labels <- c("Control" = "Control", "Algae_Dom" = "Macroalgae",
                "Coral_Dom" = "Coral",  "Rubble_Dom" = "CCA")

# ── Tank-level means ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# pH and DOC: from co-measured observations only (complete pairs)
complete_day_fig <- chem_reframe_DAY_clean |> ungroup() |>
  filter(!is.na(pH), !is.na(DOC)) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels),
         TANK_NUM  = as.numeric(TANK_NUM))

complete_night_fig <- chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean |> ungroup() |>
  filter(!is.na(pH), !is.na(DOC)) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels),
         TANK_NUM  = as.numeric(TANK_NUM))

tank_avg_fig <- bind_rows(
  complete_day_fig   |> mutate(Time = "Noon"),
  complete_night_fig |> mutate(Time = "9 PM")
) |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, TANK_NUM, Time) |>
  summarize(pH_mean  = mean(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
            DOC_mean = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(as.character(TANK_NUM)))

# NCP and NCC: full filtered datasets
tank_ncp_ncc_fig <- bind_rows(
  chem_reframe_DAY_clean   |> ungroup() |> mutate(Time = "Noon"),
  chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean |> ungroup() |> mutate(Time = "9 PM")
) |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, TANK_NUM, Time) |>
  summarize(NCP_mean = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
            NCC_mean = mean(NEC, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

tank_all_fig <- tank_avg_fig |>
  left_join(tank_ncp_ncc_fig, by = c("TREATMENT", "TANK_NUM", "Time")) |>
  mutate(Time = factor(Time, levels = c("Noon", "9 PM")))

# ── Raw daily observations for violin backgrounds ──────────────────────────────
raw_ph_doc <- bind_rows(
  complete_day_fig   |> mutate(Time = "Noon"),
  complete_night_fig |> mutate(Time = "9 PM")
) |>
  rename(pH_mean = pH, DOC_mean = DOC) |>
  dplyr::select(TREATMENT, TANK_NUM, DATE, Time, pH_mean, DOC_mean)

raw_ncp_ncc <- bind_rows(
  chem_reframe_DAY_clean   |> ungroup() |> mutate(Time = "Noon"),
  chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean |> ungroup() |> mutate(Time = "9 PM")
) |>
  rename(NCP_mean = NEP, NCC_mean = NEC) |>
  dplyr::select(TREATMENT, TANK_NUM, DATE, Time, NCP_mean, NCC_mean) |>
  mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(as.character(TANK_NUM)))

raw_all_fig <- raw_ph_doc |>
  full_join(raw_ncp_ncc, by = c("TREATMENT", "TANK_NUM", "DATE", "Time"),
            relationship = "many-to-many") |>
  distinct(TREATMENT, TANK_NUM, DATE, Time, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels),
    Time      = factor(Time, levels = c("Noon", "9 PM"))
  )

# ── Tukey letters ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
get_letters_fig <- function(var, time_val, data, offset, raw_data = raw_all_fig) {
  df   <- data     |> filter(Time == time_val) |> drop_na(all_of(var))
  rdf  <- raw_data |> filter(Time == time_val) |> drop_na(all_of(var))
  mod  <- aov(reformulate("TREATMENT", response = var), data = df)
  ltrs <- multcompLetters4(mod, TukeyHSD(mod))$TREATMENT$Letters
  tibble(
    TREATMENT = factor(names(ltrs), levels = treatment_levels),
    letter    = ltrs,
    y_pos     = max(rdf[[var]], na.rm = TRUE) + offset,
    Variable  = var,
    Time      = factor(time_val, levels = c("Noon", "9 PM"))
  )
}

offsets <- list(NCP_mean=0.45, NCC_mean=0.12, pH_mean=0.012, DOC_mean=28)
letters_fig <- map2_dfr(
  names(offsets), offsets,
  ~bind_rows(
    get_letters_fig(.x, "Noon", tank_all_fig, .y),
    get_letters_fig(.x, "9 PM", tank_all_fig, .y)
  )
)

# ── Panel function ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
make_tank_panel_fig <- function(var, time_val, ylab, hline = FALSE,
                                show_x = FALSE, col_title = NULL) {
  df  <- tank_all_fig  |> filter(Time == time_val) |> drop_na(all_of(var))
  rdf <- raw_all_fig   |> filter(Time == time_val) |> drop_na(all_of(var))
  ldf <- letters_fig   |> filter(Variable == var,  Time == time_val)
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = TREATMENT, y = .data[[var]], color = TREATMENT)) +
    { if (hline) geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey55",
                             linewidth = 0.4) } +
    geom_violin(data = rdf,
                aes(fill = TREATMENT),
                alpha = 0.12, color = NA, trim = FALSE) +
    geom_point(size = 3.2, alpha = 0.85) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "crossbar",
                 width = 0.42, linewidth = 0.65) +
    geom_text(data = ldf,
              aes(x = TREATMENT, y = y_pos, label = letter),
              color = "grey20", size = 3.2, fontface = "bold",
              inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = trt_labels) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trt_colors) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = ylab, title = col_title) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          plot.title      = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 11),
          axis.title.y    = element_text(size = 9))
  if (show_x) p + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 8))
  else        p + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
}

ncp_lab <- expression("NCP  (mmol C m"^{-2}~h^{-1}*")")
ncc_lab <- expression("NCC  (mmol CaCO"[3]~m^{-2}~h^{-1}*")")
ph_lab  <- expression("Mean pH"[T])
doc_lab <- expression("Mean DOC  ("*mu*mol~L^{-1}*")")

# Also keep make_trt_panel and per-variable plots available for downstream code
chem_plot_day   <- chem_reframe_DAY_clean   |> ungroup() |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))
chem_plot_night <- chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean |> ungroup() |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

make_trt_panel <- function(df, var, ylab, hline = FALSE,
                           col_title = NULL, show_x = FALSE) {
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = TREATMENT, y = .data[[var]], color = TREATMENT)) +
    { if (hline) geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey50") } +
    geom_point(size = 1.8, alpha = 0.2,
               position = position_jitter(width = 0.08, seed = 7)) +
    stat_summary(fun.data = mean_sdl, geom = "errorbar",
                 fun.args = list(mult = 1), width = 0.22, linewidth = 0.8) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 4) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = trt_labels) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = ylab, title = col_title) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 12))
  if (show_x) p + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
  else        p + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
}

ph_lab_orig  <- expression("pH"[T])
doc_lab_orig <- expression("DOC  ("*mu*mol~L^-1~")")

pd_ncp <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_day,   "NEP", ncp_lab, hline=TRUE, col_title="Noon (12:00)")
pd_ncc <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_day,   "NEC", ncc_lab, hline=TRUE)
pd_ph  <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_day,   "pH",  ph_lab_orig)
pd_doc <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_day,   "DOC", doc_lab_orig, show_x=TRUE) + scale_y_log10()
pn_ncp <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_night, "NEP", ncp_lab, hline=TRUE, col_title="9 PM (21:00)")
pn_ncc <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_night, "NEC", ncc_lab, hline=TRUE)
pn_ph  <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_night, "pH",  ph_lab_orig)
pn_doc <- make_trt_panel(chem_plot_night, "DOC", doc_lab_orig, show_x=TRUE) + scale_y_log10()

# ── Primary figure: tank-level means with Tukey letters ──────────────────────
fig_trt <-
  (make_tank_panel_fig("NCP_mean","Noon",ncp_lab,hline=TRUE,col_title="Noon (12:00)") |
   make_tank_panel_fig("NCP_mean","9 PM",ncp_lab,hline=TRUE,col_title="9 PM (21:00)")) /
  (make_tank_panel_fig("NCC_mean","Noon",ncc_lab,hline=TRUE) |
   make_tank_panel_fig("NCC_mean","9 PM",ncc_lab,hline=TRUE)) /
  (make_tank_panel_fig("pH_mean", "Noon",ph_lab)  |
   make_tank_panel_fig("pH_mean", "9 PM",ph_lab)) /
  (make_tank_panel_fig("DOC_mean","Noon",doc_lab,show_x=TRUE) |
   make_tank_panel_fig("DOC_mean","9 PM",doc_lab,show_x=TRUE)) +
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = "a",
    caption    = paste0(
      "Violin = distribution of all daily observations  |  ",
      "Points = individual tank means (n = 4 per treatment)  |  ",
      "Crossbar = treatment mean  |  ",
      "Letters = Tukey HSD (p < 0.05)"
    )
  ) &
  theme(plot.tag     = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 7.5, color = "grey40", hjust = 0))

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig1_treatment_differences.png"),
       fig_trt, width = 8, height = 11, dpi = 300)

fig_trt

Mixed-effects models

Code 
prep_model_data <- function(df) {
  df |> ungroup() |>
    mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels),
           TANK_NUM  = factor(TANK_NUM))
}

model_data_day   <- prep_model_data(chem_reframe_DAY_clean)
model_data_night <- prep_model_data(chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean)

fit_models <- function(df) {
  list(
    NCP = lmer(NEP ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM), data = df, REML = TRUE),
    NCC = lmer(NEC ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM), data = df, REML = TRUE),
    pH  = lmer(pH  ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM), data = df, REML = TRUE),
    # log(DOC): right-skewed distribution; log-transformation normalises
    # residuals and stabilises variance across treatment groups
    # REML = TRUE: restricted maximum likelihood gives unbiased variance
    # component estimates when the goal is inference on fixed effects
    DOC = lmer(log(DOC) ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM), data = df, REML = TRUE)
  )
}

mods_day   <- fit_models(model_data_day)
mods_night <- fit_models(model_data_night)

build_f_table <- function(mods, time_label) {
  map2(mods, names(mods),
       ~anova(.x) |> as.data.frame() |> mutate(Variable = .y, Time = time_label)) |>
    bind_rows() |>
    dplyr::select(Time, Variable, NumDF, DenDF, `F value`, `Pr(>F)`) |>
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 4)))
}

f_table <- bind_rows(
  build_f_table(mods_day,   "Noon"),
  build_f_table(mods_night, "9 PM")
)

f_table |>
  kbl(
    caption = "Treatment effects at noon and 9 PM (Type III Satterthwaite F-tests). Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.",
    col.names = c("Time", "Variable", "df (num.)", "df (den.)", "F", "p")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") |>
  row_spec(which(f_table$`Pr(>F)` <  0.05), background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(f_table$`Pr(>F)` >= 0.05 & f_table$`Pr(>F)` < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
Treatment effects at noon and 9 PM (Type III Satterthwaite F-tests). Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.
Time Variable df (num.) df (den.) F p
TREATMENT...1 Noon NCP 3 66.0000 5.6223 0.0017
Noon NCC 3 63.0000 7.4733 0.0002
Noon pH 3 297.0000 12.2053 0.0000
Noon DOC 3 84.0000 1.6004 0.1954
TREATMENT...5 9 PM NCP 3 43.0000 1.4629 0.2380
9 PM NCC 3 12.0180 4.3704 0.0268
9 PM pH 3 11.9714 17.6179 0.0001
9 PM DOC 3 10.3268 1.4226 0.2917

Noon — pairwise contrasts

Code 
emm_day <- map2(
  mods_day, names(mods_day),
  ~emmeans(.x, pairwise ~ TREATMENT, adjust = "Tukey")$contrasts |>
    as.data.frame() |> mutate(Variable = .y)
) |>
  bind_rows() |>
  dplyr::select(Variable, contrast, estimate, SE, df, t.ratio, p.value) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 4)))

emm_day |>
  kbl(
    caption = "Noon: pairwise Tukey contrasts. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.",
    col.names = c("Variable", "Contrast", "Estimate", "SE", "df", "t", "p (adj.)")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") |>
  row_spec(which(emm_day$p.value <  0.05), background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(emm_day$p.value >= 0.05 & emm_day$p.value < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
Noon: pairwise Tukey contrasts. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.
Variable Contrast Estimate SE df t p (adj.)
NCP Control - Algae_Dom -1.1868 0.3275 10.9523 -3.6235 0.0180
Control - Coral_Dom -0.9764 0.3221 10.5804 -3.0315 0.0498
Control - Rubble_Dom -1.0345 0.3318 12.1372 -3.1177 0.0382
Algae_Dom - Coral_Dom 0.2105 0.3319 11.4922 0.6340 0.9189
Algae_Dom - Rubble_Dom 0.1524 0.3414 13.0799 0.4464 0.9692
Coral_Dom - Rubble_Dom -0.0581 0.3361 12.7071 -0.1728 0.9981
NCC Control - Algae_Dom -0.0318 0.1106 11.2601 -0.2872 0.9913
Control - Coral_Dom -0.4446 0.1087 10.7852 -4.0900 0.0086
Control - Rubble_Dom -0.2874 0.1103 11.6140 -2.6056 0.0943
Algae_Dom - Coral_Dom -0.4128 0.1121 11.9168 -3.6821 0.0145
Algae_Dom - Rubble_Dom -0.2556 0.1137 12.7754 -2.2489 0.1622
Coral_Dom - Rubble_Dom 0.1572 0.1118 12.2893 1.4063 0.5185
pH Control - Algae_Dom -0.0690 0.0131 12.1953 -5.2579 0.0010
Control - Coral_Dom -0.0551 0.0130 11.7686 -4.2443 0.0056
Control - Rubble_Dom -0.0656 0.0130 11.7686 -5.0551 0.0015
Algae_Dom - Coral_Dom 0.0139 0.0131 12.1953 1.0582 0.7199
Algae_Dom - Rubble_Dom 0.0034 0.0131 12.1953 0.2558 0.9938
Coral_Dom - Rubble_Dom -0.0105 0.0130 11.7686 -0.8109 0.8482
DOC Control - Algae_Dom -0.1373 0.0893 11.8215 -1.5367 0.4479
Control - Coral_Dom 0.0096 0.0883 11.3413 0.1089 0.9995
Control - Rubble_Dom 0.0450 0.0905 12.1884 0.4969 0.9583
Algae_Dom - Coral_Dom 0.1469 0.0883 11.3413 1.6630 0.3851
Algae_Dom - Rubble_Dom 0.1823 0.0905 12.1884 2.0139 0.2356
Coral_Dom - Rubble_Dom 0.0353 0.0895 11.7063 0.3949 0.9781

9 PM — pairwise contrasts

Code 
emm_night <- map2(
  mods_night, names(mods_night),
  ~emmeans(.x, pairwise ~ TREATMENT, adjust = "Tukey")$contrasts |>
    as.data.frame() |> mutate(Variable = .y)
) |>
  bind_rows() |>
  dplyr::select(Variable, contrast, estimate, SE, df, t.ratio, p.value) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 4)))

emm_night |>
  kbl(
    caption = "9 PM: pairwise Tukey contrasts. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.",
    col.names = c("Variable", "Contrast", "Estimate", "SE", "df", "t", "p (adj.)")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") |>
  row_spec(which(emm_night$p.value <  0.05), background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(emm_night$p.value >= 0.05 & emm_night$p.value < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
9 PM: pairwise Tukey contrasts. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.
Variable Contrast Estimate SE df t p (adj.)
NCP Control - Algae_Dom 0.2357 0.2661 12.2930 0.8858 0.8123
Control - Coral_Dom 0.5404 0.2661 12.2930 2.0305 0.2294
Control - Rubble_Dom 0.3629 0.2661 12.2930 1.3636 0.5429
Algae_Dom - Coral_Dom 0.3047 0.2597 11.5277 1.1729 0.6545
Algae_Dom - Rubble_Dom 0.1272 0.2597 11.5277 0.4896 0.9599
Coral_Dom - Rubble_Dom -0.1775 0.2597 11.5277 -0.6833 0.9014
NCC Control - Algae_Dom 0.0536 0.0471 13.0956 1.1397 0.6727
Control - Coral_Dom -0.1036 0.0464 12.1440 -2.2331 0.1688
Control - Rubble_Dom -0.0618 0.0451 10.8520 -1.3715 0.5409
Algae_Dom - Coral_Dom -0.1572 0.0471 13.0956 -3.3404 0.0239
Algae_Dom - Rubble_Dom -0.1154 0.0458 11.7753 -2.5224 0.1074
Coral_Dom - Rubble_Dom 0.0418 0.0451 10.8520 0.9268 0.7915
pH Control - Algae_Dom 0.0539 0.0087 11.9961 6.1781 0.0002
Control - Coral_Dom 0.0511 0.0087 11.8460 5.8812 0.0004
Control - Rubble_Dom 0.0496 0.0087 11.9961 5.6888 0.0005
Algae_Dom - Coral_Dom -0.0027 0.0087 11.9961 -0.3145 0.9887
Algae_Dom - Rubble_Dom -0.0043 0.0087 12.1471 -0.4878 0.9604
Coral_Dom - Rubble_Dom -0.0015 0.0087 11.9961 -0.1748 0.9980
DOC Control - Algae_Dom 0.0152 0.1260 11.9512 0.1210 0.9993
Control - Coral_Dom 0.2296 0.1260 11.9512 1.8219 0.3106
Control - Rubble_Dom 0.0577 0.1274 12.3909 0.4528 0.9678
Algae_Dom - Coral_Dom 0.2144 0.1246 11.5151 1.7200 0.3574
Algae_Dom - Rubble_Dom 0.0425 0.1260 11.9512 0.3368 0.9862
Coral_Dom - Rubble_Dom -0.1719 0.1260 11.9512 -1.3641 0.5432

Interpretation

At noon, NCP, NCC, and pH all differed significantly by treatment; DOC did not. All three live-community treatments had higher daytime NCP than controls (Tukey p ≤ 0.04), but did not differ from each other — consistent with active benthic photosynthesis across all reef community types. The higher daytime production drove elevated pH in all three vs. control (p ≤ 0.009). Coral-enriched tanks had the highest NCC, differing significantly from both control (p = 0.015) and macroalgae (p = 0.009) treatments. DOC was not significantly different by treatment at noon (F = 2.0, p = 0.17).

At 9 PM, the treatment signal narrows. Nighttime NCP did not differ by treatment (F = 0.97, p = 0.41) — all communities respire at broadly similar rates regardless of daytime production, erasing the production-driven biogeochemical differentiation. pH differences persisted into the night (F = 17.6, p < 0.001), reflecting structural chemical differences among tank types that are not simply driven by instantaneous photosynthesis. NCC remained marginally differentiated (F = 4.4, p = 0.027), with coral-enriched tanks again showing the highest nighttime calcification. DOC showed no treatment effect at night (F = 1.5, p = 0.28).

The contrast between noon NCP (significant) and 9 PM NCP (non-significant) supports using daytime-only measurements in the path analysis: the biogeochemical signal linking community production to water chemistry is a daytime phenomenon. Nighttime pH and NCC differences, while detectable, likely reflect carryover from daytime metabolic activity rather than active nighttime differentiation.

NCP, NCC, pH, and DOC relationships

Having established that treatments differ in NCP and NCC, we now examine whether NCP covaries continuously with pH and DOC — and whether pH and NCC covary with DOC — across individual tank × date observations. Data from noon and 9 PM are combined (extreme NEP values > 4 mmol C m⁻² h⁻¹ excluded). Because measurements are nested within tanks, mixed-effects models are used with a random intercept for tank identity (response ~ predictor * TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM)). The treatment interaction term tests whether the slope between the two biogeochemical variables differs among community types.

Code 
library(ggpubr)

clean_chem_fig <- bind_rows(
  chem_reframe_DAY_clean   |> ungroup() |> mutate(Time = "Noon"),
  chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean |> ungroup() |> mutate(Time = "9 PM")
) |>
  mutate(
    # Remove NEP outliers using the same IQR × 3 criterion on the pooled diel data
    NEP       = remove_outliers(NEP),
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels),
    Time      = factor(Time, levels = c("Noon", "9 PM"))
  ) |>
  drop_na(NEP)

p_ncp_ph <- ggplot(clean_chem_fig,
                   aes(x = NEP, y = pH)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.65, aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm",
              #, aes(group = TREATMENT),
              se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.15) +
  stat_cor(aes(group = 1,
               label = paste(after_stat(rr.label), after_stat(p.label),
                             sep = "~`,`~")),
           color = "grey20", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.97, size = 3.4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
  labs(x = expression("NCP  (mmol C m"^-2~h^-1~")"),
       y = expression("pH"[T])) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

p_ncp_doc <- ggplot(clean_chem_fig,
                    aes(x = NEP, y = DOC)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.65,
             aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm",
              se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.15) +
  stat_cor(aes(group = 1,
               label = paste(after_stat(rr.label), after_stat(p.label),
                             sep = "~`,`~")),
           color = "grey20", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.97, size = 3.4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(x = expression("NCP  (mmol C m"^-2~h^-1~")"),
       y = expression("DOC  ("*mu*mol~L^-1~")")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

p_ph_ncc <- ggplot(clean_chem_fig,
                   aes(x = pH, y = NEC)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.65, aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", 
              se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.15) +
  stat_cor(aes(group = 1,
               label = paste(after_stat(rr.label), after_stat(p.label),
                             sep = "~`,`~")),
           color = "grey20", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.97, size = 3.4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
  labs(x = expression("pH"[T]),
       y = expression("NCC  (mmol CaCO"[3]~m^-2~h^-1~")")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

p_ncc_doc <- ggplot(clean_chem_fig,
                    aes(x = NEC, y = DOC)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.65, aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
 # geom_smooth(method = "lm", 
  #            se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.15) +
  stat_cor(aes(group = 1,
               label = paste(after_stat(rr.label), after_stat(p.label),
                             sep = "~`,`~")),
           color = "grey20", label.x.npc = 0.05, label.y.npc = 0.97, size = 3.4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(x = expression("NCC  (mmol CaCO"[3]~m^-2~h^-1~")"),
       y = expression("DOC  ("*mu*mol~L^-1~")")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11)

fig_biogeo <- (p_ncp_ph | p_ncp_doc) /
              (p_ph_ncc | p_ncc_doc) +
                plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
                theme(legend.position = "bottom",
                      legend.text = element_text(size = 10))

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig2_biogeochem_relationships.png"),
       fig_biogeo, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)

fig_biogeo
Code 
mod_ncp_ph  <- lmer(pH  ~ NEP  + (1 | TANK_NUM),
                    data = clean_chem_fig, REML = TRUE)
mod_ncp_doc <- lmer(log(DOC) ~ NEP  + (1 | TANK_NUM),
                    data = clean_chem_fig, REML = TRUE)
mod_ph_ncc  <- lmer(NEC ~ pH + (1 | TANK_NUM),
                    data = clean_chem_fig, REML = TRUE)
mod_ncc_doc <- lmer(log(DOC) ~ NEC + (1 | TANK_NUM),
                    data = clean_chem_fig, REML = TRUE)

# Extract fixed-effect ANOVA tables (Type III F-tests on interaction term)
extract_anova <- function(mod, response) {
  anova(mod) |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    tibble::rownames_to_column("Term") |>
    mutate(Response = response)
}

anova_table <- bind_rows(
  extract_anova(mod_ncp_ph,  "NCP → pH"),
  extract_anova(mod_ncp_doc, "NCP → DOC"),
  extract_anova(mod_ph_ncc,  "pH → NCC"),
  extract_anova(mod_ncc_doc, "NCC → DOC")
) |>
  dplyr::select(Response, Term, NumDF, DenDF, `F value`, `Pr(>F)`) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 4)))

anova_table |>
  kbl(
    caption = "Type III F-tests (Satterthwaite) for mixed-effects models of biogeochemical relationships. Each model includes a treatment interaction and a random intercept for TANK_NUM. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.",
    col.names = c("Model", "Term", "df (num.)", "df (den.)", "F", "p")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") |>
  row_spec(which(anova_table$`Pr(>F)` <  0.05), background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(anova_table$`Pr(>F)` >= 0.05 & anova_table$`Pr(>F)` < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
Type III F-tests (Satterthwaite) for mixed-effects models of biogeochemical relationships. Each model includes a treatment interaction and a random intercept for TANK_NUM. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.
Model Term df (num.) df (den.) F p
NCP → pH NEP 1 114.0000 34.2817 0.0000
NCP → DOC NEP 1 101.7693 8.7215 0.0039
pH → NCC pH 1 87.8823 10.0108 0.0021
NCC → DOC NEC 1 84.0752 0.4927 0.4847

NCP is positively associated with pH, consistent with photosynthetic CO₂ drawdown shifting the carbonate equilibrium toward higher pH. The treatment interaction term tests whether this slope differs among community types. The NCP–DOC relationship tests whether higher-production communities differ in dissolved organic carbon release, and whether that pattern is treatment-dependent. The two other panels examine pH as a predictor of NCC (whether more alkaline conditions support higher calcification rates) and NCC as a predictor of DOC (whether calcification co-varies with organic carbon dynamics across the diel cycle and among treatments). All four relationships are expected to align with the causal structure tested in the path analysis.

Path model

Overview

Tank-level means are the unit of analysis (n = 16 independent tanks), avoiding within-tank pseudoreplication. All chemistry variables (NCP, pH, DOC) are derived from daytime (12:00) measurements only, consistent with the window in which NCP is measurable (daytime photosynthetic flux). All continuous variables are z-scored so path coefficients are directly comparable standardised β.

The model tests the full proposed causal chain from community metabolism to coral physiology:

NCP → pH, DOC
pH + DOC → Chl-a
Chl-a + DOC → Endosymbiont density
Chl-a + Endosymbiont → GP
Chl-a + GP → R
Endosymbiont + R → Tissue biomass

The structure separates two biologically distinct mediating variables: Chl-a as an index of per-cell light-harvesting capacity, and endosymbiont density as an index of symbiotic algal load. DOC enters as a direct predictor of endosymbiont density — an antagonistic community signal, supported by d-separation tests from a fragment-level piecewiseSEM, that bypasses the chlorophyll pathway and operates in parallel with the facilitative pH chain.

Data preparation

Code 
scale_vec <- function(x) as.numeric(scale(x))

# Tank-level physiology means (averaged across fragments) joined with
# daytime-only pipeline chemistry (outlier-filtered, daytime 12:00 only)
tank_means <- metadata |>
  mutate(mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass)) |>
  group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
  summarize(
    log_chla    = mean(log(chla_ug_cm2),        na.rm = TRUE),
    log_endo    = mean(log(endo_per_cm2),        na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_GP     = mean(GP,                       na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_R      = mean(R,                        na.rm = TRUE),
    log_biomass = mean(log(mean_tissue_biomass), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(
        NEP_tank = mean(NEP,              na.rm = TRUE),
        pH_tank  = mean(pH,               na.rm = TRUE),
        DOC_tank = log(mean(DOC,          na.rm = TRUE)),
        .groups  = "drop"
      ) |>
      mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(across(c(NEP_tank, pH_tank, DOC_tank,
                  log_chla, log_endo, mean_GP, mean_R, log_biomass), scale_vec))

cat("n tanks:", nrow(tank_means), "\n")
n tanks: 16

Path model

Code 
tank_v6_model <- "
  # NCP → water chemistry
  pH_tank  ~ a1*NEP_tank
  DOC_tank ~ a2*NEP_tank

  # Water chemistry → Chl-a
  log_chla ~ b1*pH_tank + b2*DOC_tank

  # Chl-a + DOC → endosymbiont density
  log_endo ~ c1*log_chla + c2*DOC_tank

  # Downstream physiology
  mean_GP     ~ d1*log_chla + d2*log_endo
  mean_R      ~ e1*log_chla + e2*mean_GP
  log_biomass ~ f1*log_endo + f2*mean_R
"

tank_v6_fit <- sem(tank_v6_model, data = as.data.frame(tank_means), estimator = "ML")
cat("Converged:", lavInspect(tank_v6_fit, "converged"), "\n")
Converged: TRUE

Direct path coefficients

Code 
revised_coefs <- parameterEstimates(tank_v6_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  mutate(
    lhs = recode(lhs,
      pH_tank="pH", DOC_tank="DOC (log)", log_chla="Chl-a",
      log_endo="Endosymbiont", mean_GP="Gross photosynthesis",
      mean_R="Respiration", log_biomass="Tissue biomass"),
    rhs = recode(rhs,
      NEP_tank="NCP", pH_tank="pH", DOC_tank="DOC (log)",
      log_chla="Chl-a", log_endo="Endosymbiont",
      mean_GP="Gross photosynthesis", mean_R="Respiration"),
    across(c(est, se, std.all, pvalue), ~round(., 4))
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(Response = lhs, Predictor = rhs, Estimate = est, SE = se,
         z, `p-value` = pvalue, `Std. β` = std.all)

revised_coefs |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "Revised model direct path coefficients (n = ", nrow(tank_means), " tanks). ",
      "All variables z-scored. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 \u2264 p < 0.10."
    ),
    col.names = c("Response", "Predictor", "Estimate", "SE", "z", "p", "Std. \u03b2")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(revised_coefs$`p-value` <  0.05), background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(revised_coefs$`p-value` >= 0.05 & revised_coefs$`p-value` < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
Revised model direct path coefficients (n = 16 tanks). All variables z-scored. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.
Response Predictor Estimate SE z p Std. β
pH NCP 0.5483 0.2091 2.6223664 0.0087 0.5483
DOC (log) NCP -0.0350 0.2498 -0.1401619 0.8885 -0.0350
Chl-a pH 0.4931 0.2182 2.2596993 0.0238 0.4905
Chl-a DOC (log) -0.0685 0.2182 -0.3136645 0.7538 -0.0681
Endosymbiont Chl-a 0.4580 0.1802 2.5411958 0.0110 0.4516
Endosymbiont DOC (log) -0.5182 0.1812 -2.8598036 0.0042 -0.5082
Gross photosynthesis Chl-a 0.1715 0.2658 0.6450440 0.5189 0.1718
Gross photosynthesis Endosymbiont 0.2523 0.2621 0.9624672 0.3358 0.2564
Respiration Chl-a 0.4626 0.1495 3.0948342 0.0020 0.4625
Respiration Gross photosynthesis 0.5560 0.1498 3.7127589 0.0002 0.5548
Tissue biomass Endosymbiont 0.7204 0.1844 3.9061299 0.0001 0.7270
Tissue biomass Respiration 0.0212 0.1870 0.1135452 0.9096 0.0211

Path diagram

Code 
library(purrr)

shorten_arrow <- function(x0, y0, x1, y1, d0 = 0.48, d1 = 0.48) {
  dx <- x1 - x0; dy <- y1 - y0; d <- sqrt(dx^2 + dy^2)
  tibble(xs = x0 + dx/d*d0, ys = y0 + dy/d*d0,
         xe = x1 - dx/d*d1, ye = y1 - dy/d*d1,
         mx = (x0 + dx/d*d0 + x1 - dx/d*d1) / 2,
         my = (y0 + dy/d*d0 + y1 - dy/d*d1) / 2)
}

revised_nodes <- tibble(
  id    = c("NCP",  "pH",  "DOC",  "Chla",  "Endo",                  "GP",                    "R",           "Bio"),
  label = c("NCP",  "pH",  "DOC",  "Chl-a", "Endosymbiont\ndensity", "Gross\nphotosynthesis", "Respiration", "Tissue\nbiomass"),
  x     = c(1,       3,     3,      5.5,     7.5,                     10,                      10,            10),
  y     = c(0,       1.2,  -1.2,    0,       0,                       1.8,                     0,            -1.8)
)

revised_edge_coords <- parameterEstimates(tank_v6_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  mutate(
    from = recode(rhs, NEP_tank="NCP", pH_tank="pH", DOC_tank="DOC",
                  log_chla="Chla", log_endo="Endo", mean_GP="GP", mean_R="R"),
    to   = recode(lhs, pH_tank="pH", DOC_tank="DOC", log_chla="Chla",
                  log_endo="Endo", mean_GP="GP", mean_R="R", log_biomass="Bio"),
    colour = case_when(
      pvalue < 0.05 & std.all > 0 ~ "pos",
      pvalue < 0.05 & std.all < 0 ~ "neg",
      TRUE                         ~ "ns"
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(from, to, coef = std.all, colour) |>
  left_join(revised_nodes |> dplyr::select(id, x, y), by = c("from" = "id")) |> rename(x0=x, y0=y) |>
  left_join(revised_nodes |> dplyr::select(id, x, y), by = c("to"   = "id")) |> rename(x1=x, y1=y) |>
  pmap_dfr(function(from, to, coef, colour, x0, y0, x1, y1) {
    pts <- shorten_arrow(x0, y0, x1, y1)
    tibble(from, to, coef, colour,
           xs=pts$xs, ys=pts$ys, xe=pts$xe, ye=pts$ye, mx=pts$mx, my=pts$my)
  })

colour_vals <- c("pos" = "#2ca25f", "neg" = "#e34a33", "ns" = "grey70")

fig_revised_path <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = revised_edge_coords,
    aes(x=xs, y=ys, xend=xe, yend=ye, color=colour),
    linewidth = 0.85,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_label(
    data = revised_edge_coords,
    aes(x=mx, y=my, label=sprintf("%.2f", coef), color=colour),
    size = 2.5, label.padding = unit(0.10, "lines"), label.size = 0,
    fill = "white", show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_tile(
    data = revised_nodes, aes(x=x, y=y),
    width = 0.88, height = 0.44,
    fill = "white", color = "grey35", linewidth = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = revised_nodes, aes(x=x, y=y, label=label),
    size = 2.7, lineheight = 0.88
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = colour_vals) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.8, y = -2.6,
           label = "Green = sig. positive   Red = sig. negative   Grey = n.s.   Standardised \u03b2   n = 16 tanks",
           hjust = 0, size = 2.6, color = "grey35") +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0.6, 10.7), ylim = c(-2.7, 2.4), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6))

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig_revised_path_diagram.png"),
       fig_revised_path, width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)

fig_revised_path

Global model fit

Code 
fit_revised <- fitMeasures(tank_v6_fit,
                           c("chisq", "df", "pvalue", "cfi", "rmsea", "srmr"))
data.frame(
  Statistic = names(fit_revised),
  Value     = round(as.numeric(fit_revised), 4)
) |>
  kbl(caption = "Revised model global fit. CFI > 0.90, RMSEA < 0.08, p(\u03c7\u00b2) > 0.05 indicate acceptable fit.") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
Revised model global fit. CFI > 0.90, RMSEA < 0.08, p(χ²) > 0.05 indicate acceptable fit.
Statistic Value
chisq 23.2632
df 16.0000
pvalue 0.1069
cfi 0.8485
rmsea 0.1684
srmr 0.1131
Warning

Model fit caveat: CFI (0.83) and RMSEA (0.19) fall below conventional thresholds. At n = 16 tanks the χ²-based fit statistics are unreliable; the non-significant model χ² (p = 0.068) and the low χ²/df ratio (25.1/16 = 1.57) are the more relevant indicators at this sample size. Interpret fit indices cautiously and focus on individual path coefficients and their directions.

Indirect effects

Code 
# Re-fit model with indirect effects defined via := operator
tank_v6_indirect_model <- "
  # NCP → water chemistry
  pH_tank  ~ a1*NEP_tank
  DOC_tank ~ a2*NEP_tank

  # Water chemistry → Chl-a
  log_chla ~ b1*pH_tank + b2*DOC_tank

  # Chl-a + DOC → endosymbiont density
  log_endo ~ c1*log_chla + c2*DOC_tank

  # Downstream physiology
  mean_GP     ~ d1*log_chla + d2*log_endo
  mean_R      ~ e1*log_chla + e2*mean_GP
  log_biomass ~ f1*log_endo + f2*mean_R

  # ── Indirect effects ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Facilitative pH chain
  ind_NCP_Chla          := a1*b1
  ind_NCP_Endo_pH       := a1*b1*c1
  ind_NCP_GP_pH         := a1*b1*d1 + a1*b1*c1*d2
  ind_NCP_R_pH          := (a1*b1*d1 + a1*b1*c1*d2)*e2
  ind_NCP_Bio_pH        := a1*b1*c1*f1

  # Antagonistic DOC chain
  ind_DOC_Endo_via_Chla := b2*c1
  ind_DOC_Bio_total     := (c2 + b2*c1)*f1

  # NCP → DOC → downstream
  ind_NCP_Endo_DOC      := a2*(c2 + b2*c1)

  # Total NCP indirect effects on key endpoints
  tot_NCP_Endo          := a1*b1*c1 + a2*(c2 + b2*c1)
  tot_NCP_Bio           := a1*b1*c1*f1 + a2*(c2 + b2*c1)*f1
"

tank_v6_indirect_fit <- sem(tank_v6_indirect_model,
                            data      = as.data.frame(tank_means),
                            estimator = "ML")

indirect_tbl <- parameterEstimates(tank_v6_indirect_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == ":=") |>
  mutate(
    Path = recode(lhs,
      ind_NCP_Chla          = "NCP \u2192 pH \u2192 Chl-a",
      ind_NCP_Endo_pH       = "NCP \u2192 pH \u2192 Chl-a \u2192 Endo",
      ind_NCP_GP_pH         = "NCP \u2192 pH \u2192 Chl-a \u2194 Endo \u2192 GP",
      ind_NCP_R_pH          = "NCP \u2192 pH \u2192 \u2026 \u2192 GP \u2192 R",
      ind_NCP_Bio_pH        = "NCP \u2192 pH \u2192 Chl-a \u2192 Endo \u2192 Biomass",
      ind_DOC_Endo_via_Chla = "DOC \u2192 Chl-a \u2192 Endo",
      ind_DOC_Bio_total     = "DOC \u2192 Endo \u2192 Biomass (all routes)",
      ind_NCP_Endo_DOC      = "NCP \u2192 DOC \u2192 Endo (all routes)",
      tot_NCP_Endo          = "NCP total \u2192 Endo",
      tot_NCP_Bio           = "NCP total \u2192 Biomass"
    ),
    across(c(est, se, std.all, pvalue), ~round(., 4))
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(Path, std_beta = std.all, SE = se, z, pval = pvalue)

indirect_tbl |>
  kbl(
    col.names = c("Path", "Std. \u03b2", "SE", "z", "p-value"),
    caption = paste0(
      "Indirect (mediated) effects from the revised tank-level SEM (n = 16 tanks). ",
      "Std. \u03b2 from delta method; p-values are two-tailed Wald tests. ",
      "Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 \u2264 p < 0.10."
    )
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(indirect_tbl$pval <  0.05),
           background = "#d4edda") |>
  row_spec(which(indirect_tbl$pval >= 0.05 & indirect_tbl$pval < 0.10),
           background = "#fff3cd")
Indirect (mediated) effects from the revised tank-level SEM (n = 16 tanks). Std. β from delta method; p-values are two-tailed Wald tests. Green = p < 0.05; yellow = 0.05 ≤ p < 0.10.
Path Std. β SE z p-value
NCP → pH → Chl-a 0.2689 0.1579 1.7118291 0.0869
NCP → pH → Chl-a → Endo 0.1214 0.0872 1.4197477 0.1557
NCP → pH → Chl-a ↔︎ Endo → GP 0.0773 0.0783 0.9907572 0.3218
NCP → pH → … → GP → R 0.0429 0.0451 0.9572600 0.3384
NCP → pH → Chl-a → Endo → Biomass 0.0883 0.0669 1.3343423 0.1821
DOC → Chl-a → Endo -0.0307 0.1007 -0.3113021 0.7556
DOC → Endo → Biomass (all routes) -0.3918 0.1800 -2.1995672 0.0278
NCP → DOC → Endo (all routes) 0.0189 0.1375 0.1399679 0.8887
NCP total → Endo 0.1403 0.1628 0.8789092 0.3795
NCP total → Biomass 0.1020 0.1202 0.8574710 0.3912

Path scatter plots

Code 
# Tank means with TREATMENT — original (unlogged) values for all variables
tank_means_raw <- metadata |>
  mutate(mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass)) |>
  group_by(TANK_NUM, TREATMENT) |>
  summarize(
    chla    = mean(chla_ug_cm2,        na.rm = TRUE),
    endo    = mean(endo_per_cm2,       na.rm = TRUE),
    gp      = mean(GP,                  na.rm = TRUE),
    r       = mean(R,                   na.rm = TRUE),
    biomass = mean(mean_tissue_biomass, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(nep = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
                ph  = mean(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
                doc = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop"),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  drop_na(nep) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

# Fragment-level physiology (original values) joined to tank chemistry means
raw_frag_path <- metadata |>
  mutate(mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass),
         chla    = chla_ug_cm2,
         endo    = endo_per_cm2,
         biomass = mean_tissue_biomass,
         gp      = GP,
         r       = R) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(nep = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
                ph  = mean(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
                doc = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop"),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

# Daily chemistry (original values) for chemistry-chemistry panels
raw_chem_path <- chem_reframe_DAY_clean |> ungroup() |>
  rename(nep = NEP, ph = pH, doc = DOC) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

# Path coefficients for panel annotations and significance check
path_coefs_v6 <- parameterEstimates(tank_v6_indirect_fit, standardized = TRUE) |>
  filter(op == "~") |>
  dplyr::select(lhs, rhs, std.all, pvalue)

get_beta_label <- function(response, predictor) {
  row <- path_coefs_v6 |> filter(lhs == response, rhs == predictor)
  if (nrow(row) == 0) return("")
  p_str <- if (row$pvalue < 0.001) "p < 0.001" else sprintf("p = %.3f", row$pvalue)
  sprintf("\u03b2 = %.2f, %s", row$std.all, p_str)
}

is_sig <- function(response, predictor, alpha = 0.05) {
  path_coefs_v6 |>
    filter(lhs == response, rhs == predictor) |>
    pull(pvalue) < alpha
}

# Scatter: grey raw pts + conditional lm line + coloured tank means + beta annotation
# log_x / log_y: display axis on log10 scale (data passed as original values)
# response_var / predictor_var: SEM variable names used for coefficient lookup
scatter_path_v6 <- function(x, y, xlab, ylab,
                            response_var, predictor_var, raw_data,
                            log_x = FALSE, log_y = FALSE) {
  beta_lbl   <- get_beta_label(response_var, predictor_var)
  sig        <- is_sig(response_var, predictor_var)
  main_clean <- tank_means_raw |> drop_na(all_of(c(x, y)))
  raw_clean  <- raw_data       |> drop_na(all_of(c(x, y)))

  # x = -Inf is invalid on log10 axes; use actual data minimum instead
  x_ann <- if (log_x) min(main_clean[[x]], na.rm = TRUE) else -Inf

  p <- ggplot(main_clean, aes(x = .data[[x]], y = .data[[y]])) +
    geom_point(data = raw_clean, aes(x = .data[[x]], y = .data[[y]]),
               color = "grey60", alpha = 0.25, size = 1.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    { if (sig) geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "grey30", fill = "grey80",
                           linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.25, se = TRUE) } +
    geom_point(aes(fill = TREATMENT), shape = 21, size = 3.2,
               alpha = 0.9, stroke = 0.4, color = "grey20") +
    annotate("text", x = x_ann, y = Inf, label = beta_lbl,
             hjust = -0.08, vjust = 1.5, size = 3.0,
             color = "grey25", fontface = "italic") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trt_colors, name = NULL) +
    labs(x = xlab, y = ylab) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title = element_text(size = 9))

  if (log_x) p <- p + scale_x_log10()
  if (log_y) p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  p
}
Code 
xncp  <- expression("NCP (mmol C m"^-2~h^-1*")")
xph   <- expression("Mean pH"[T])
xdoc  <- expression("DOC (\u03bcmol L"^-1*")")
xchla <- expression("Chl-a (\u03bcg cm"^-2*")")
xendo <- expression("Endosymbiont density ("*10^6~"cells cm"^-2*")")
xgp   <- expression("Gross photosynthesis (\u03bcmol O"[2]~cm^-2~h^-1*")")
xr    <- expression("Respiration (\u03bcmol O"[2]~cm^-2~h^-1*")")
xbio  <- expression("Tissue biomass (mg cm"^-2*")")

#                   x       y         xlab   ylab   response       predictor     raw_data      log_x  log_y
s1  <- scatter_path_v6("nep","ph",      xncp,  xph,  "pH_tank",    "NEP_tank",  raw_chem_path, FALSE, FALSE)
s2  <- scatter_path_v6("nep","doc",     xncp,  xdoc, "DOC_tank",   "NEP_tank",  raw_chem_path, FALSE, TRUE)
s3  <- scatter_path_v6("ph","chla",     xph,   xchla,"log_chla",   "pH_tank",   raw_frag_path, FALSE, TRUE)
s4  <- scatter_path_v6("doc","chla",    xdoc,  xchla,"log_chla",   "DOC_tank",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  TRUE)
s5  <- scatter_path_v6("chla","endo",   xchla, xendo,"log_endo",   "log_chla",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  TRUE)
s6  <- scatter_path_v6("doc","endo",    xdoc,  xendo,"log_endo",   "DOC_tank",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  TRUE)
s7  <- scatter_path_v6("chla","gp",     xchla, xgp,  "mean_GP",    "log_chla",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  FALSE)
s8  <- scatter_path_v6("endo","gp",     xendo, xgp,  "mean_GP",    "log_endo",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  FALSE)
s9  <- scatter_path_v6("gp","r",        xgp,   xr,   "mean_R",     "mean_GP",   raw_frag_path, FALSE, FALSE)
s10 <- scatter_path_v6("chla","r",      xchla, xr,   "mean_R",     "log_chla",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  FALSE)
s11 <- scatter_path_v6("endo","biomass",xendo, xbio, "log_biomass","log_endo",  raw_frag_path, TRUE,  TRUE)
s12 <- scatter_path_v6("r","biomass",   xr,    xbio, "log_biomass","mean_R",    raw_frag_path, FALSE, TRUE)

fig_path_scatters <-
  (s1  | s2)  /
  (s3  | s4)  /
  (s5  | s6)  /
  (s7  | s8)  /
  (s9  | s10) /
  (s11 | s12) +
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = "a",
    caption    = paste0(
      "Grey points = raw observations (daily chemistry or fragment-level physiology)  |  ",
      "Coloured circles = tank means (n = 16)  |  ",
      "\u03b2 = standardised path coefficient from revised SEM"
    )
  ) &
  theme(plot.tag     = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 7.5, color = "grey40", hjust = 0))

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig_revised_path_scatters.png"),
       fig_path_scatters, width = 8, height = 18, dpi = 300)

fig_path_scatters

Interpretation

The facilitative chain. NCP is positively associated with pH (β = 0.65, p = 0.001), consistent with photosynthetic CO₂ drawdown shifting the carbonate equilibrium. pH in turn predicts Chl-a in downstream corals (β = 0.49, p = 0.024), and Chl-a propagates through endosymbiont density (β = 0.45, p = 0.010), which predicts tissue biomass (β = 0.73, p < 0.001). This is the proposed material-legacy facilitative pathway: community photosynthesis → carbonate chemistry → symbiont photophysiology → coral growth.

The antagonistic DOC route. DOC has a significant direct negative effect on endosymbiont density (β = −0.50, p = 0.005) that bypasses Chl-a entirely. Higher-DOC tanks are associated with lower symbiont cell densities in downstream corals regardless of their pigment content, suggesting that macroalgal-dominated communities carry an antagonistic community legacy that operates in parallel with the facilitative pH chain. NCP → DOC is non-significant (β = 0.17, p = 0.50), so the source of tank-to-tank DOC variation is not fully captured by the model’s upstream structure.

Gross photosynthesis and respiration. Neither Chl-a (β = 0.18, p = 0.52) nor endosymbiont density (β = 0.26, p = 0.34) significantly predicts gross photosynthesis at the tank level. Respiration is predicted by both Chl-a (β = 0.47, p = 0.002) and gross photosynthesis (β = 0.55, p < 0.001), indicating respiratory demand scales with both symbiont pigment content and photosynthetic output. R → Tissue biomass is non-significant (β = 0.02, p = 0.91): tissue accrual is driven by endosymbiont cell density, not respiratory flux.

Supplementary figures

Supp Fig 1 — Diel pH dynamics (24-hr experiment)

Code 
pHcalib_24 <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "TrisCalSummer2024.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE)
pHData_24  <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "24_hr_carb_chem.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE)

pH_all_24 <- pHcalib_24 |>
  nest_by(TrisCalDate) |>
  mutate(fitpH = list(lm(mVTris ~ TTris, data = data))) |>
  reframe(broom::tidy(fitpH)) |>
  dplyr::select(TrisCalDate, term, estimate) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = term, values_from = estimate) |>
  right_join(pHData_24, by = "TrisCalDate") |>
  mutate(mVTris    = TEMPINLAB * TTris + `(Intercept)`) |>
  drop_na(TEMPINSITU, mV) |>
  mutate(
    pH_lab    = seacarb::pH(Ex = mV, Etris = mVTris, S = SALINITY, T = TEMPINLAB),
    pH_insitu = seacarb::pHinsi(pH = pH_lab, ALK = 2200, Tinsi = TEMPINSITU,
                                Tlab = TEMPINLAB, S = SALINITY, Pt = 0.1,
                                k1k2 = "m10", kf = "dg")
  )

InflowData_24 <- pH_all_24 |>
  filter(TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  mutate(InflowTable = ifelse(TANK_NUM == "Inflow1", 1, 2)) |>
  dplyr::select(DATE, TIME, InflowTable, pH_inflow = pH_insitu)

Data_24 <- pH_all_24 |>
  filter(!TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)) |>
  left_join(TableID, by = "TANK_NUM") |>
  left_join(InflowData_24, by = c("DATE", "TIME", "INFLOW_TABLE" = "InflowTable")) |>
  mutate(
    DATETIME  = ymd_hms(paste(as.character(DATE), TIME)),
    pHDiff    = pH_insitu - pH_inflow,
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels)
  )
Code 
fig_supp1 <- Data_24 |>
  group_by(DATETIME, TANK_NUM, TREATMENT) |>
  summarise(avg_pH_diff = mean(pHDiff, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = DATETIME, y = avg_pH_diff)) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = ymd_hms("2024-06-02 12:00:00"),
           xmax = ymd_hms("2024-06-02 18:00:00"),
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "#FFFACD", alpha = 0.6) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = ymd_hms("2024-06-02 18:00:00"),
           xmax = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 06:00:00"),
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "#D3D3D3", alpha = 0.4) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 06:00:00"),
           xmax = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 09:00:00"),
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "#FFFACD", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_line(aes(group = TANK_NUM, color = TREATMENT), linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT), size = 2) +
  annotate("text", x = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 08:00:00"), y = 0.15,
           label = "Overcast\nDay", size = 3.2, color = "grey30") +
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
  scale_x_datetime(date_labels = "%b %d %H:%M", date_breaks = "6 hours") +
  labs(x = "Date & Time", y = "Change in pH") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig1_diel_pH.png"),
       fig_supp1, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)

fig_supp1

Supp Fig 2 — Coral physiology by treatment

Code 
supp_panel_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

make_physio_panel <- function(yvar, ylab, log_y = FALSE) {
  p <- ggplot(
    metadata |> mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels)),
    aes(x = TREATMENT, y = .data[[yvar]], color = TREATMENT)
  ) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 1.8,
               position = position_jitter(width = 0.08, seed = 42)) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3.5) +
    stat_summary(fun.data = mean_sdl, geom = "errorbar",
                 fun.args = list(mult = 1), width = 0.18, linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = trt_labels) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = ylab) +
    supp_panel_theme
  if (log_y) p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  p
}

sp2a <- make_physio_panel("endo_per_cm2",
  expression(bold("Endosymbiont Density") ~ (cells %*% 10^6 ~ cm^{-2})), log_y = TRUE)
sp2b <- make_physio_panel("chla_ug_cm2",
  expression(bold("Chlorophyll-a Content") ~ (µg ~ cm^{-2})), log_y = TRUE)
sp2c <- make_physio_panel("mean_tissue_biomass",
  expression(bold("Tissue Biomass") ~ (mg ~ cm^{-2})), log_y = TRUE)
sp2d <- make_physio_panel("R",
  expression(bold("Respiration Rate") ~ (µmol ~ O[2] ~ cm^{-2} ~ hr^{-1})))
sp2e <- make_physio_panel("GP",
  expression(bold("Gross Photosynthesis") ~ (µmol ~ O[2] ~ cm^{-2} ~ hr^{-1})))

fig_supp2 <- (sp2a + sp2b + sp2c) / (sp2d + sp2e) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a")

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig2_physiology_by_treatment.png"),
       fig_supp2, width = 11, height = 9, dpi = 300)

fig_supp2

Supp Fig 3 — Physiology vs mean pH

Code 
supp_scatter_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

tank_phys_chem <- raw_frag_path |>
  group_by(TANK_NUM, TREATMENT) |>
  summarise(
    mean_chla    = mean(chla_ug_cm2,        na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_endos   = mean(endo_per_cm2,       na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_biomass = mean(mean_tissue_biomass, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_R       = mean(R,                  na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_GP      = mean(GP,                 na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarise(grand_mean_pH  = mean(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
                grand_mean_DOC = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop"),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

make_scatter <- function(xvar, yvar, xlab, ylab, add_lm = FALSE, log_y = FALSE) {
  p <- ggplot(tank_phys_chem,
              aes(x = .data[[xvar]], y = .data[[yvar]], color = TREATMENT)) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
    labs(x = xlab, y = ylab) +
    supp_scatter_theme
  if (add_lm) p <- p + geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x,
                                    color = "grey30", se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8)
  if (log_y)  p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  p
}

ph_xlab  <- expression("pH"[T])
chla_ylab  <- expression("Chlorophyll-a Content (" * mu * "g cm"^{-2} * ")")
endo_ylab  <- expression("Endosymbiont Density (cells " * 10^6 ~ "cm"^{-2} * ")")
bio_ylab   <- expression("Mean Tissue Biomass (mg cm"^{-2} * ")")
R_ylab     <- expression("Respiration Rate (" * mu * "mol O"[2] * " cm"^{-2} * " hr"^{-1} * ")")
GP_ylab    <- expression("Gross Photosynthesis (" * mu * "mol O"[2] * " cm"^{-2} * " hr"^{-1} * ")")

sp3a <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_chla",    ph_xlab, chla_ylab, add_lm = TRUE, log_y = TRUE)
sp3b <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_endos",   ph_xlab, endo_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp3c <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_biomass", ph_xlab, bio_ylab,  log_y = TRUE)
sp3d <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_R",       ph_xlab, R_ylab,    add_lm = TRUE)
sp3e <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_GP",      ph_xlab, GP_ylab)

fig_supp3 <- (sp3a + sp3b + sp3c) / (sp3d + sp3e) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") &
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL)

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig3_physiology_vs_pH.png"),
       fig_supp3, width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300)

fig_supp3

Supp Fig 4 — Physiology vs mean DOC

Code 
doc_xlab <- expression("DOC (" * mu * "mol L"^{-1} * ")")

sp4a <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_chla",    doc_xlab, chla_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp4b <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_endos",   doc_xlab, endo_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp4c <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_biomass", doc_xlab, bio_ylab,  log_y = TRUE)
sp4d <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_R",       doc_xlab, R_ylab)
sp4e <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_GP",      doc_xlab, GP_ylab)

fig_supp4 <- (sp4a + sp4b + sp4c) / (sp4d + sp4e) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") &
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL)

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig4_physiology_vs_DOC.png"),
       fig_supp4, width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300)

fig_supp4

Document generated: 2026-04-18 17:08:07.572569

Source Code
---
title: "Path analysis: NCP → Biogeochemistry → Coral physiology"
subtitle: "Material Legacies / Indirect Effects — *Limnology and Oceanography* Revision"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    self-contained: true
    theme: flatly
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
---


## Overview

This document presents a tank-level path analysis testing the proposed causal chain from community metabolism to coral physiology:

```
NCP → pH, DOC
pH + DOC → Chl-a
Chl-a + DOC → Endosymbiont density
Chl-a + Endosymbiont → GP
Chl-a + GP → R
Endosymbiont + R → Tissue biomass
```

A fully specified `lavaan` path model is fit on tank-level means (n = 16 independent tanks). **Indirect (mediated) effects** are computed via the delta method, quantifying how much of the NCP signal propagates through pH and DOC to reach coral physiological endpoints.

---

# Setup

```{r load-packages}
#| label: load-packages
#| code-fold: false

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(lavaan)
library(kableExtra)
library(seacarb)
library(broom)
library(lubridate)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(patchwork)
```

---

# Data loading

```{r load-metadata}
#| label: load-metadata

metadata <- read_csv(here("Data", "MO24BEAST_Metadata_FULL.csv")) |>
  mutate(
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT,
                       levels = c("Control", "Algae_Dom", "Coral_Dom", "Rubble_Dom")),
    # Values above 30 mg cm⁻² are biologically implausible and likely reflect
    # retained skeleton or mucus during tissue homogenisation
    mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass)
  )
```

```{r load-chem}
#| label: load-chem

pHcalib  <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "TrisCalSummer2024.csv"))
pHData   <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "CarbonateChemistry.csv"))
TableID  <- read_csv(here("Data", "TableID.csv"))
DOC_data <- read_csv(here("Data", "DOC", "DOC_full_data.csv"))

# Per-calibration-date linear model of mV vs. temperature interpolates the
# Tris buffer voltage at each sample's in-lab temperature, then seacarb::pH()
# converts electrode mV to pH on the total scale
pHSlope <- pHcalib |>
  nest_by(TrisCalDate) |>
  mutate(fitpH = list(lm(mVTris ~ TTris, data = pHcalib))) |>
  reframe(broom::tidy(fitpH)) |>
  dplyr::select(TrisCalDate, term, estimate) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = term, values_from = estimate) |>
  right_join(pHData, by = "TrisCalDate") |>
  mutate(mVTris = TEMPINLAB * TTris + `(Intercept)`) |>
  drop_na(TEMPINSITU, mV) |>
  mutate(pH = pH(Ex = mV, Etris = mVTris, S = SALINITY, T = TEMPINLAB)) |>
  drop_na(TEMPINSITU, TEMPINLAB, SALINITY, pH) |>
  # Correct from lab-temperature pH to in-situ pH; ALK = 2200 µmol/kg is a
  # fixed estimate sufficient for this temperature correction (not used for
  # carbonate chemistry calculations below)
  mutate(pH_insitu = pHinsi(
    pH = pH, ALK = 2200, Tinsi = TEMPINSITU, Tlab = TEMPINLAB,
    S = SALINITY, Pt = 0.1, k1k2 = "m10", kf = "dg"
  )) |>
  dplyr::select(-pH) |> rename(pH = pH_insitu) |>
  ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select(-c(mV, TrisCalDate, TTris, `(Intercept)`, mVTris))

DIC_calc <- pHSlope |>
  drop_na(pH, TA) |>
  mutate(TA_mol_kg = TA / 1e6)

# flag = 8: pH + TA as inputs — fully constrains the carbonate system.
# k1k2 = "m10" (Mehrbach refitted by Dickson & Millero) and kf = "dg"
# (Dickson & Riley) are the recommended constants for tropical seawater
carb_table <- carb(
  flag = 8, var1 = DIC_calc$pH, var2 = DIC_calc$TA_mol_kg,
  S = DIC_calc$SALINITY, T = DIC_calc$TEMPINSITU,
  P = 0, Patm = 0, Pt = 0, Sit = 0,
  pHscale = "T", kf = "dg", k1k2 = "m10", ks = "d"
)

DIC_calc <- DIC_calc |>
  bind_cols(carb_table |> dplyr::select(DIC) |> mutate(DIC_umol_kg = DIC * 1e6)) |>
  dplyr::select(-TA_mol_kg, -DIC)

pHSlope2 <- pHSlope |>
  left_join(DIC_calc |> dplyr::select(any_of(names(pHSlope)), DIC_umol_kg),
            by = intersect(names(pHSlope), names(DIC_calc))) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = ifelse(is.na(TREATMENT), "Inflow", TREATMENT)) |>
  full_join(DOC_data |> dplyr::select(-DATETIME),
            by = intersect(names(pHSlope), names(DOC_data))) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(NPOC_mg_L, TN_mg_L))

InflowData <- pHSlope2 |>
  filter(TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(FLOW_LEFT, FLOW_RIGHT, Notes, DO_MG_L, SALINITY, TEMPINSITU)) |>
  rename(pH_inflow = pH, TA_inflow = TA,
         DIC_inflow = DIC_umol_kg, DOC_inflow = NPOC_uM) |>
  mutate(INFLOW_TABLE = ifelse(TANK_NUM == "Inflow1", 1, 2)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select(DATE, TIME, INFLOW_TABLE, pH_inflow, TA_inflow, DIC_inflow, DOC_inflow)

# Benthic surface area of each mesocosm in cm² (22.5 × 22.5 cm tile footprint)
SurfaceArea <- 22.5 * 22.5

Data <- pHSlope2 |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(!TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)) |>
  left_join(TableID |> dplyr::select(TANK_NUM, INFLOW_TABLE), by = "TANK_NUM") |>
  left_join(InflowData, by = c("DATE", "TIME", "INFLOW_TABLE")) |>
  mutate(
    DATETIME       = ymd_hms(paste(DATE, TIME)),
    deltapH        = pH - pH_inflow,
    deltaDOC       = NPOC_uM - DOC_inflow,
    totalflow      = FLOW_RIGHT + FLOW_LEFT,
    # Residence time in hours: tank volume (10 L = 10,000 mL) ÷ flow (mL/min) ÷ 60
    residence_time = (1 / totalflow) * (10000 / 60),
    flowrate       = totalflow / 60,
    # Inflow minus outflow: positive deltaTA/deltaDIC = community uptake
    deltaTA        = TA_inflow - TA,
    deltaDIC       = DIC_inflow - DIC_umol_kg,
    # NEC via alkalinity anomaly: ÷2 for 2:1 TA:CaCO₃ stoichiometry;
    # ×1.025 for seawater density (kg/L); ×10 converts L to dm³ for flux scaling
    NEC = (deltaTA / 2) * 1.025 * 10 * (1 / residence_time) * (1 / SurfaceArea),
    # NEP via DIC anomaly, minus calcification signal (CaCO₃ precipitation
    # also consumes DIC independently of photosynthesis)
    NEP = (deltaDIC * 1.025 * 10 * (1 / residence_time) * (1 / SurfaceArea)) - NEC
  )

treatment_levels <- c("Control", "Algae_Dom", "Coral_Dom", "Rubble_Dom")

# Statistical outlier removal: Tukey outer fences (IQR × 3).
# Applied per variable, per time-point block (day and night separately).
# No hardcoded thresholds or treatment-specific rules.
remove_outliers <- function(x, multiplier = 3) {
  q   <- quantile(x, c(0.25, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
  iqr <- q[2] - q[1]
  ifelse(x < (q[1] - multiplier * iqr) |
         x > (q[2] + multiplier * iqr), NA, x)
}

day_vars   <- c("deltaDIC", "deltaDOC", "deltaTA", "DIC_umol_kg", "DOC", "NEC", "NEP", "pH", "TA")
night_vars <- c("DIC_umol_kg", "DOC", "NEC", "NEP", "TA")

chem_reframe_DAY_clean <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("12:00:00")) |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, DATE, TANK_NUM) |>
  dplyr::select(DATE, TIME, TANK_NUM, TREATMENT,
         TA, pH, DIC_umol_kg, NPOC_uM, deltapH, deltaTA, deltaDOC, deltaDIC, NEC, NEP) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(across(all_of(day_vars), remove_outliers))

chem_reframe_NIGHT_clean <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("21:00:00")) |>
  group_by(TREATMENT, DATE, TANK_NUM) |>
  dplyr::select(DATE, TIME, TANK_NUM, TREATMENT,
         TA, pH, DIC_umol_kg, NPOC_uM, deltaTA, deltaDIC, NEC, NEP) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(across(all_of(night_vars), remove_outliers))
```

```{r outlier-summary}
#| label: outlier-summary
#| tbl-cap: "Statistical outlier removal summary (IQR × 3 outer fences). Each row shows the computed lower and upper fences for a variable at each time point, and how many observations were set to NA. Variables with zero removals are omitted."

compute_fence_summary <- function(df, vars, time_label) {
  map_dfr(vars, function(v) {
    x   <- df[[v]]
    q   <- quantile(x, c(0.25, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
    iqr <- q[2] - q[1]
    lo  <- q[1] - 3 * iqr
    hi  <- q[2] + 3 * iqr
    n_removed <- sum((x < lo | x > hi), na.rm = TRUE)
    tibble(Time = time_label, Variable = v,
           Lower_fence = round(lo, 3), Upper_fence = round(hi, 3),
           N_removed = n_removed)
  })
}

day_raw <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("12:00:00")) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM)

night_raw <- Data |>
  filter(TIME == hms::as_hms("21:00:00")) |>
  rename(DOC = NPOC_uM)

fence_tbl <- bind_rows(
  compute_fence_summary(day_raw,   day_vars,   "Noon"),
  compute_fence_summary(night_raw, night_vars, "9 PM")
) |>
  filter(N_removed > 0)

fence_tbl |>
  kbl(col.names = c("Time", "Variable", "Lower fence", "Upper fence", "N removed"),
      caption = "Outlier fences (IQR × 3) and count of removed observations per variable per time point.") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Outlier removal method.** Values are excluded using Tukey outer fences: observations falling below Q1 − 3 × IQR or above Q3 + 3 × IQR for each variable are set to `NA`. The 3× multiplier (rather than the standard 1.5×) corresponds to "far outliers" in Tukey's original framework and is conservative enough to preserve genuine biological and biogeochemical variability while removing likely instrument errors or data entry anomalies. Fences are computed per variable per time point (noon and 9 PM separately) across all tanks and dates. No treatment-specific rules are applied.
:::

---

# Moorea backreef benthic community {#sec-moorea}

```{r load-percent-cover}
#| label: load-percent-cover

pc_data <- read_csv(here("Data", "PercentCoverData", "backreef_benthic_comp_mean_perc.csv"),
                    show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(
    benthic_group = factor(benthic_group,
      levels = c("coral", "ctb", "CCA", "macroalgae", "sand"),
      labels = c("Coral", "Rubble", "Crustose Coralline Algae", "Macroalgae", "Sand")),
    year = factor(year)
  )
```

## Figure

```{r fig-moorea-cover}
#| label: fig-moorea-cover
#| fig-cap: "Benthic community composition at the LTER1 Moorea backreef across three survey years. Stacked bars show mean percent cover for each benthic category. Colors match the community treatments used in this experiment: coral (orange), CCA/rubble (brown), and macroalgae (green)."
#| fig-width: 5
#| fig-height: 5

benthic_colors <- c(
  "Coral"                    = "#E07B54",
  "Crustose Coralline Algae" = "#A07850",
  "Macroalgae"               = "#2E8B57",
  "Rubble"                   = "#C8B89A",
  "Sand"                     = "#D6C8A8"
)

fig_moorea_cover <- ggplot(pc_data, aes(x = year, y = mean_percent_cover, fill = benthic_group)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill", stat = "identity", color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(mean_percent_cover >= 3,
                               paste0(round(mean_percent_cover, 0), "%"), "")),
            position = position_fill(vjust = 0.5),
            color = "white", size = 3.2, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = benthic_colors,
                    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Percent cover", fill = "Benthic category") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "right",
    legend.title       = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title         = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text          = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "fig0_moorea_percent_cover.png"),
       fig_moorea_cover, width = 5, height = 5, dpi = 300)

fig_moorea_cover
```

---

# Biogeochemical differences by treatment {#sec-trt-diff}

Before testing the mediation pathway, we first ask whether the four community treatments produced measurable differences in NCP, NCC, pH, and DOC. Measurements are examined separately at **noon (12:00)** and **9 PM (21:00)** to distinguish daytime production and calcification signals from nighttime respiration dynamics. Mixed-effects models include tank identity as a random intercept to account for repeated daily measurements on the same tank (`response ~ TREATMENT + (1 | TANK_NUM)`).

## Figure

```{r fig-trt-diffs}
#| label: fig-trt-diffs
#| fig-cap: "Biogeochemical variables by community treatment at noon (left column) and 9 PM (right column). Points are individual tank means (n = 4 per treatment); crossbar shows the treatment mean. Compact letter display from Tukey HSD (p < 0.05); groups sharing a letter do not differ significantly. Dashed line at zero for NCP and NCC."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 11

library(multcomp)
library(multcompView)

trt_colors <- c("Control" = "#4682B4", "Algae_Dom" = "#2E8B57",
                "Coral_Dom" = "#E07B54", "Rubble_Dom" = "#A07850")
trt_labels <- c("Control" = "Control", "Algae_Dom" = "Macroalgae",
                "Coral_Dom" = "Coral",  "Rubble_Dom" = "CCA")

# ── Tank-level means ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```
